market_size.reasoning: TAM = APAC knowledge-worker count (48M) × note-tool ARPU (S$10/mo) × 12. SAM = SG+MY+ID mid/SME subset (9.2M workers × S$10 × 12). SOM = 5% of SAM over 3 years.
market_size.implication_for_us: SOM S$4.6M/yr is meaningful but not winner-takes-all; we compete on wedge specificity, not scale.

policies.title: PSG grant for productivity SaaS
policies.body: Up to 50% support for pre-approved productivity tools listed by IMDA.
policies.implication_for_us: IMDA pre-approval halves effective price for SG SMEs, doubling WTP ceiling.

policies.title: PDPA Section 13 amendments
policies.body: Mandatory data breach notification within 72h; SG residency preferred for sensitive categories.
policies.implication_for_us: SG residency becomes a differentiator against global vendors whose default is US/EU.

policies.title: DPP tax treatment of cross-border SaaS
policies.body: 8% withholding on SaaS invoices paid to non-SG vendors for SG corporates.
policies.implication_for_us: Global competitors' effective price is 8% higher for SG corporates; use in sales.

cultural_signals.observation: SG SMEs prefer founder-led WhatsApp support over ticketing systems.
cultural_signals.evidence: 68% of SG SMEs cite WhatsApp as primary B2B channel (ASME 2025 survey).
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Offer WhatsApp-first support in onboarding; build WhatsApp Business integration.

economic_signals.implication_for_us: Tight budgets; anchor pricing on productivity ROI, not convenience.

adoption_patterns.note: SMEs lag on tool standardisation, not willingness.

trends.title: AI-native note tools becoming default
trends.evidence: Notion AI, Mem.ai, Reflect all ship AI-first; 62% of new paid seats include AI add-on.
trends.implication_for_us: AI integration is table stakes, not differentiator.
